title: keystone-operator Helm Values
description: Schema for the keystone-operator Helm chart values (CC-0069)

cidr.description: IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR block (e.g., '10.96.0.1/32', '2001:db8::/32'). IPv4 octets are bounded 0-255 to surface obvious typos (e.g. '999.0.0.1/32') at helm render time rather than as opaque kubectl apply admission errors. The IPv6 half of the pattern is intentionally loose — it matches any hex+colon sequence and does not enforce valid RFC 4291 group counts or '::' rules — because a schema-complete IPv6 regex is disproportionately complex for the stated goal of catching obvious typos; final IPv6 validation is delegated to kubectl/apiserver admission of the rendered NetworkPolicy (CC-0090, REQ-005).

stringMap.description: Map of string keys to string values (Kubernetes labels/selectors)

image.description: Container image configuration for the operator

tag.description: Container image tag; defaults to .Chart.AppVersion when left empty

resources.description: Resource requests and limits for the operator container

limits.description: Resource limits for the operator container

requests.description: Resource requests for the operator container

namespaceScoped.description: Deploy with namespace-scoped Role/RoleBinding instead of ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding (CC-0043). Restricts the operator to its release namespace. Requires webhook.enabled=false.

leaderElection.description: Leader election configuration for high availability

enabled.description: Enable leader election for high availability
Enable admission webhooks for Keystone CR validation and defaulting

port.description: Port for the Prometheus metrics endpoint

create.description: Create a service account for the operator

name.description: Name of the service account; if empty and create is true, a name is generated from the fullname template

networkPolicy.description: NetworkPolicy for operator pod egress/ingress restriction (CC-0090). Opt-in via enabled=true.

enabled.description: Render a NetworkPolicy that default-denies operator pod traffic except for the explicit rules below (CC-0090, REQ-001)

kubeApiServer.description: Kubernetes API server reachability. Required (non-empty) when enabled=true — fail-closed (CC-0090, REQ-004, REQ-006)

cidrs.description: List of kube-apiserver CIDR blocks the operator may reach for egress and receive webhook calls from

ports.description: List of kube-apiserver TCP ports the operator may reach (e.g., 443, 6443)

dns.description: DNS egress configuration for kube-dns resolution (CC-0090, REQ-003)

enabled.description: Permit UDP+TCP/53 egress to the DNS peer below

namespaceSelector.description: namespaceSelector matchLabels for the DNS peer (defaults to kube-system). minProperties:1 prevents an empty map from silently broadening the DNS peer to match all namespaces (CC-0090, REQ-005).

podSelector.description: podSelector matchLabels for the DNS peer (defaults to k8s-app=kube-dns). minProperties:1 prevents an empty map from silently broadening the DNS peer to match all pods (CC-0090, REQ-005).

allowMetricsFrom.description: List of NetworkPolicyPeer objects permitted to scrape the metrics port. Empty disables metrics ingress (CC-0090, REQ-008)

webhookClients.description: Webhook admission ingress source override. Only consulted when webhook.enabled=true (CC-0090, REQ-007)

cidrs.description: CIDR blocks permitted to call the webhook on 9443. When empty, falls back to kubeApiServer.cidrs

nameOverride.description: Override the chart name used in resource names

fullnameOverride.description: Override the full resource name prefix, skipping release-name prefixing
